name: Brik
ingredients: tuna, egg, parsley, harissa, onion, capers, brik pastry sheets, oil
quantities: 1 can tuna, 1 egg, 2 tbsp parsley, 1 tsp harissa, 1 onion, 1 tbsp capers, 4 sheets, oil for frying
steps: Mix tuna, chopped onion, parsley, capers and harissa. Place mixture on brik sheet. Add raw egg. Fold sheet into triangle. Fry in hot oil until golden.
description: Classic Tunisian crispy pastry filled with tuna and egg.

name: Lablabi
ingredients: chickpeas, harissa, garlic, cumin, lemon, olive oil, stale bread, egg, tuna
quantities: 400g chickpeas, 2 tsp harissa, 4 cloves garlic, 1 tsp cumin, 1 lemon, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 slices bread, 2 eggs, 1 can tuna
steps: Boil chickpeas with garlic and cumin. Place stale bread in bowl. Pour chickpea broth over bread. Add harissa, lemon juice, olive oil. Top with egg and tuna.
description: Traditional Tunisian street food soup with chickpeas and harissa.

name: Couscous Tunisien
ingredients: couscous, lamb, chickpeas, carrots, zucchini, tomato paste, harissa, onion, olive oil, ras el hanout
quantities: 500g couscous, 500g lamb, 200g chickpeas, 3 carrots, 2 zucchini, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 tbsp harissa, 1 onion, 4 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp ras el hanout
steps: Brown lamb with onion and spices. Add tomato paste and harissa. Add vegetables and chickpeas with water. Steam couscous. Serve couscous topped with meat and vegetables.
description: The iconic Tunisian couscous with lamb and vegetables.

name: Ojja
ingredients: eggs, merguez, tomatoes, peppers, harissa, garlic, olive oil, cumin, caraway
quantities: 4 eggs, 4 merguez sausages, 4 tomatoes, 2 peppers, 1 tsp harissa, 3 cloves garlic, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp caraway
steps: Fry merguez in olive oil. Add garlic, peppers and tomatoes. Add harissa and spices. Simmer until sauce thickens. Break eggs into sauce. Cover and cook until eggs set.
description: Spicy Tunisian egg and merguez shakshuka.

name: Mechouia Salad
ingredients: grilled peppers, grilled tomatoes, grilled onion, garlic, tuna, olives, capers, olive oil, lemon, harissa
quantities: 4 peppers, 4 tomatoes, 1 onion, 3 cloves garlic, 1 can tuna, 10 olives, 1 tbsp capers, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 lemon, 1 tsp harissa
steps: Grill peppers, tomatoes and onion directly on flame. Peel and chop finely. Mix with garlic, olive oil, lemon and harissa. Top with tuna, olives and capers.

name: Harissa
ingredients: dried red chilies, garlic, coriander seeds, caraway seeds, olive oil, salt, lemon juice
quantities: 200g dried chilies, 6 cloves garlic, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tsp caraway, 4 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, 1 lemon
steps: Soak dried chilies in hot water for 30 minutes. Drain and blend with garlic, spices, salt and lemon juice. Gradually add olive oil while blending until smooth paste forms.
description: The iconic Tunisian chili paste used in almost every dish.

name: Keftaji
ingredients: lamb mince, peppers, tomatoes, potatoes, eggs, harissa, garlic, parsley, olive oil, cumin
quantities: 300g lamb mince, 2 peppers, 2 tomatoes, 2 potatoes, 2 eggs, 1 tsp harissa, 3 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp parsley, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin
steps: Fry potatoes until golden. Fry peppers and tomatoes separately. Form lamb into small meatballs and fry. Scramble eggs lightly. Chop everything together in pan. Mix with harissa and spices.
description: Tunisian mixed fry of meat, vegetables and eggs.

name: Asida
ingredients: semolina, water, butter, honey, olive oil, salt
quantities: 500g semolina, 1L water, 50g butter, 3 tbsp honey, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt
steps: Boil salted water. Gradually add semolina while stirring continuously. Cook on low heat for 20 minutes stirring constantly. Add butter. Serve with honey and olive oil drizzled on top.
description: Traditional Tunisian semolina porridge served with honey.

name: Bambalouni
ingredients: flour, yeast, sugar, salt, water, oil for frying, powdered sugar
quantities: 500g flour, 7g yeast, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp salt, 300ml warm water, oil for frying, powdered sugar to serve
steps: Mix flour, yeast, sugar and salt. Add warm water and knead until smooth. Rest for 1 hour. Shape into rings. Deep fry until golden brown. Drain and coat with powdered sugar.
description: Tunisian street food donuts coated in powdered sugar.

name: Chorba Frik
ingredients: frik (green wheat), lamb, tomatoes, onion, chickpeas, celery, parsley, olive oil, ras el hanout, harissa
quantities: 200g frik, 300g lamb, 3 tomatoes, 1 onion, 100g chickpeas, 2 stalks celery, 3 tbsp parsley, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp ras el hanout, 1 tsp harissa
steps: Brown lamb with onion in olive oil. Add tomatoes, chickpeas and spices. Cover with water and simmer 30 minutes. Add frik and celery. Cook 20 more minutes. Garnish with parsley.
description: Traditional Tunisian green wheat soup especially popular during Ramadan.

name: Makroudh
ingredients: semolina, dates, orange blossom water, olive oil, honey, cinnamon, anise seeds
quantities: 500g semolina, 400g dates, 3 tbsp orange blossom water, 150ml olive oil, 200ml honey, 1 tsp cinnamon, 1 tsp anise seeds
steps: Mix semolina with olive oil and water to form dough. Make date filling with dates, cinnamon and orange blossom water. Shape dough around filling into diamond shapes. Deep fry until golden. Dip in warm honey.
description: Traditional Tunisian semolina pastry filled with dates and soaked in honey.

name: Felfel Mehchi
ingredients: bell peppers, minced meat, rice, tomatoes, onion, parsley, harissa, olive oil, cumin, tomato paste
quantities: 6 peppers, 300g minced meat, 100g rice, 2 tomatoes, 1 onion, 3 tbsp parsley, 1 tsp harissa, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 2 tbsp tomato paste
steps: Cut tops off peppers and remove seeds. Mix meat with rice, onion, tomatoes, parsley and spices. Stuff peppers with mixture. Place in baking dish with tomato sauce. Bake at 180C for 45 minutes.
description: Tunisian stuffed peppers with spiced meat and rice.

name: Kafteji
ingredients: zucchini, peppers, tomatoes, potatoes, eggs, harissa, garlic, olive oil, cumin, salt
quantities: 2 zucchini, 2 peppers, 2 tomatoes, 2 potatoes, 3 eggs, 1 tsp harissa, 2 cloves garlic, 4 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, salt to taste
steps: Fry all vegetables separately in olive oil until cooked. Scramble eggs lightly. Chop all ingredients together roughly. Season with harissa, garlic and cumin. Serve warm.
description: Vegetarian version of the Tunisian mixed fried vegetables with eggs.

name: Marqa Zitoune
ingredients: chicken, olives, preserved lemon, onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, olive oil, parsley, coriander
quantities: 1 whole chicken, 200g green olives, 1 preserved lemon, 1 onion, 4 cloves garlic, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp turmeric, 4 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp parsley, 2 tbsp coriander
steps: Brown chicken pieces with onion and garlic. Add spices and water. Simmer 40 minutes. Add olives and preserved lemon slices. Cook 15 more minutes. Garnish with fresh herbs.
description: Tunisian chicken stew with olives and preserved lemon.

name: Tagine Malsouka
ingredients: malsouka pastry, minced meat, eggs, cheese, parsley, onion, harissa, olive oil, cumin
quantities: 8 malsouka sheets, 300g minced meat, 4 eggs, 100g cheese, 3 tbsp parsley, 1 onion, 1 tsp harissa, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin
steps: Cook minced meat with onion and spices. Beat eggs and mix with cheese and parsley. Layer malsouka sheets in oiled pan. Add meat mixture. Pour egg mixture over. Bake at 180C for 30 minutes until golden.
description: Tunisian baked pastry tagine with meat and eggs.
